body.title: Consequence check: Granny concurrency gate + stop command
body.result: GRANNY_THROTTLED: 0 in recent logs (old code removed THROTTLED posting; no queue backlog observed); STOP command not needed since spawn path eliminated. Effect: clear.
body.description:
**What changed:** Granny now enforces cc_max_concurrent=1 before dispatch; CC.0 stop command kills all cc-T-* sessions reliably.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) GRANNY_THROTTLED events may mask legitimate dispatch failures — a ticket that should dispatch looks like throttling; (2) stop_cc_minions may race with a session that just committed and is about to close cleanly — killing it mid-close-ticket could leave a ticket in_progress; (3) cc_max_concurrent=1 means a large queue drains slowly — Granny may appear stalled if a single CC session takes a long time.
**Signals to watch:** GRANNY_THROTTLED events in channel; in_progress tickets that should have been closed but weren't after stop; queue depth growing faster than it drains.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15 or after 10+ dispatch cycles observed.

**Worst-case token spend:**
  - Per-failure cost: GRANNY_THROTTLED fires with no dispatch — zero inference tokens, just a channel post
  - Hard cap: cc_max_concurrent=1 enforced in code; no dispatch fires if any cc-T-* session exists
  - Blast radius: if check fails (tmux returns wrong count), worst case is 2 concurrent sessions — same as today minus the runaway

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
